content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA, consisting of NNN integers.

Consider the following definitions:


Prefix xor array of an array AAA is defined as the array BBB such that Bi=A1⊕…⊕AiB_i = A_1 \oplus \ldots \oplus A_iBi​=A1​⊕…⊕Ai​, where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes bitwise XOR operation.

In other words, B=[A1,A1⊕A2,…,A1⊕A2…⊕AN]B=[A_1, A_1 \oplus A_2, \ldots, A_1 \oplus A_2 \ldots \oplus A_N]B=[A1​,A1​⊕A2​,…,A1​⊕A2​…⊕AN​]
The value of an array AAA is the number of distinct values in array BBB. For example, for array A=[1,2,3,0]A=[1, 2, 3, 0]A=[1,2,3,0], we have B=[1,3,0,0]B=[1, 3, 0, 0]B=[1,3,0,0]. The array BBB has 333 distinct values, thus, the value of array AAA is 333.
One right shift on the array AAA is a transformation that changes the array A=[A1,A2…,AN]A=[A_1, A_2 \ldots, A_N]A=[A1​,A2​…,AN​] to A′=[AN,A1,…,AN−1]A^{'}=[A_N, A_1, \ldots, A_{N-1}]A′=[AN​,A1​,…,AN−1​].
Calculate the **maximum** value of the array AAA you can get, by performing any (possibly zero) number of right shifts on the array AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input contains TTT −-− the number of test cases you need to solve.
The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN −-− the size of the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,…,ANA_1, \ldots, A_NA1​,…,AN​ −-− the elements of the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of an array AAA you can achieve after performing any (possibly zero) number of right shifts on the array.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
**Subtask 1 (20 points):** Sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 200020002000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Perform zero right shifts on A=[0,0]A=[0,0]A=[0,0]. Thus, B=[0,0⊕0]=[0,0]B= [0, 0\oplus0]=[0,0]B=[0,0⊕0]=[0,0]. Thus, the value of array AAA is 111.
Test case 222: Perform two right shifts on A=[1,1,1,2,2,2]A = [1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2]A=[1,1,1,2,2,2]. Thus, AAA becomes [2,2,1,1,1,2][2, 2, 1, 1, 1, 2][2,2,1,1,1,2]. The array B=[2,0,1,0,1,3]B = [2, 0, 1, 0, 1, 3]B=[2,0,1,0,1,3]. Thus, the value of AAA is 444. It can be shown that the value of AAA cannot exceed 444.
Test case 333: Perform three right shifts on A=[1,2,2,8]A = [1, 2, 2, 8]A=[1,2,2,8]. Thus, AAA becomes [2,2,8,1][2, 2, 8, 1][2,2,8,1]. The array B=[2,2⊕2,2⊕2⊕8,2⊕2⊕8⊕1]=[2,0,8,9]B = [2, 2\oplus 2, 2\oplus 2\oplus 8, 2\oplus 2\oplus 8\oplus 1] = [2, 0, 8, 9]B=[2,2⊕2,2⊕2⊕8,2⊕2⊕8⊕1]=[2,0,8,9]. Thus, the value of AAA is 444. It can be shown that the value of AAA cannot exceed 444.
